clusters.headline: US kills leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang in air strike, Trump says
clusters.summary: Niño Guerrero was killed in a "swift and lethal kinetic strike," the US President wrote in a social media post.

clusters.headline: British forces intercept Russian shadow fleet oil tanker attempting to cross English Channel
Trump says deal to end Iran war will be signed Sunday, as Iran disagrees on timing
clusters.summary: Pakistan's prime minister, a key mediator in U.S.-Iran talks to end the war, said Saturday that a peace deal was closer "than ever before," and could be finalized "in the next 24 hours."

clusters.headline: Author Chimamanda Adichie accuses hospital of stalling review into son's death
clusters.summary: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie's 21-month old son died in a Lagos hospital, which she says is trying to stop an inquest into the circumstances of his death.

clusters.headline: 2026 World Cup: Australia stun Turkiye 2-0 in counterattacking masterclass
clusters.summary: Australia secure win in Group D courtesy of goals from Nestory Irankunda and Connor Metcalfe.

clusters.headline: Switzerland votes on right-wing bid to cap country’s population
clusters.summary: A 'yes' vote would force the government to restrict asylum, residency permits, and scrap EU deal on free movement.

clusters.headline: Kidnapped Nigerian retired general dies in captivity
clusters.summary: Maj Gen Rabe Abubakar's death highlights continuing security challenges facing parts of the country.

clusters.headline: Senegal: Institut Pasteur de Dakar Sequences the First Human Whole Genomes in the Country and Launches an Early Cancer Detection Platform
clusters.summary: [Institut Pasteur de Dakar] First 40 human whole genomes sequenced using Illumina and Oxford Nanopore Technologies platforms Institut Pasteur de Dakar launches clinical laboratory and bioinformatics platform, enabling testing for predisposition to early-onset breast cancer in Senegal and other rare

clusters.headline: Fast-track border checks, return centers, 'mandatory solidarity': What the EU’s new asylum rules could change — and what they may not.
clusters.summary: Germany aims to further reduce the number of new asylum seekers. The reformed EU asylum law (CEAS) came into effect on Friday, but experts say it is unlikely to achieve all it sets out to do.

clusters.headline: Nigeria: Democracy Day - Tinubu Says Economic Reforms Restoring Stability, Pledges Greater Prosperity for Nigerians
clusters.summary: [This Day] President Bola Ahmed Tinubu on Friday defended his administration's economic reforms, saying they were necessary to rescue Nigeria from fiscal strain and lay the foundation for long-term prosperity.

clusters.headline: I-dle Syncopation Singapore concert: K-pop queens show off immense charisma, powerful vocals and a JJ Lin cover
clusters.summary: K-pop girl group I-dle staged the Singapore leg of their Syncopation tour on Saturday (Jun 13) at the Singapore Indoor Stadium.

clusters.headline: Vincent's parents 'never say he's good enough' - so he turned to a middle-aged couple online
clusters.summary: A niche group of content creators called "virtual parents" have become hugely popular among young people.
